timeline.title: Birth
timeline.description: Lionel Andrés Messi was born in Rosario, Santa Fe Province, Argentina.

timeline.title: Family Environment
timeline.description: Raised in a close-knit working-class family. His father, Jorge Messi, worked in a steel factory and coached youth football, while his mother, Celia María Cuccittini, worked in a magnet manufacturing workshop.

timeline.title: Started Playing Football
timeline.description: Began playing football with local club Grandoli, where his father Jorge served as coach.

timeline.title: Joined Newell's Old Boys
timeline.description: Joined the youth academy of Newell's Old Boys and became a member of the famous 'Machine of '87' youth generation.

timeline.title: Diagnosed with Growth Hormone Deficiency
timeline.description: Diagnosed with Growth Hormone Deficiency (GHD). Daily hormone treatment became necessary but was financially difficult for the family.

timeline.title: Continued Youth Development
timeline.description: Remained one of the standout youth players at Newell's Old Boys despite medical challenges.

timeline.title: Barcelona Trial
timeline.description: Traveled to Barcelona for a trial after being recommended to the club. Impressed coaches immediately with his performances.

timeline.title: Napkin Contract
timeline.description: Carles Rexach agreed to sign Messi on a paper napkin, promising Barcelona would recruit him. The document became one of the most famous contracts in football history.

timeline.title: Moved to Spain
timeline.description: Relocated with his family to Barcelona after the club agreed to cover his medical treatment and development.

timeline.title: Arrival in Barcelona
timeline.description: Messi arrived in Barcelona to begin a trial after impressing scouts who had been informed of his exceptional talent.

timeline.title: First Training Session
timeline.description: Completed his first training session with Barcelona's youth players and immediately impressed coaches, teammates and club officials.

timeline.title: Napkin Contract Signed
timeline.description: Sporting director Carles Rexach signed the famous paper napkin agreement committing Barcelona to sign Messi and support his development and medical treatment.

timeline.title: Joined La Masia
timeline.description: Officially entered FC Barcelona's youth system and began playing for the Infantil B team while continuing treatment for Growth Hormone Deficiency.

timeline.title: Rapid Progress Through Youth Teams
timeline.description: Adapted quickly to Spanish football and became one of the standout talents in Barcelona's academy.

timeline.title: Promotion to Cadete Teams
timeline.description: Advanced through Barcelona's Cadete levels, consistently producing goals and assists while gaining recognition as one of the academy's brightest prospects.

timeline.title: Promotion to Juvenil A
timeline.description: Reached Barcelona's top youth side and continued his rapid development against older opponents.

timeline.title: Barcelona C Debut
timeline.description: Made his official debut for Barcelona C, beginning his progression into senior football within the club.

timeline.title: Unofficial First-Team Debut
timeline.description: Played for Barcelona's first team in a friendly against FC Porto during the inauguration of the Estádio do Dragão, becoming one of the youngest players to feature for the senior side.

timeline.title: Barcelona B Promotion
timeline.description: Earned promotion to Barcelona B after impressive performances for the youth teams and Barcelona C.

timeline.title: Fast-Tracked to the First Team
timeline.description: Manager Frank Rijkaard began integrating Messi into first-team training, recognizing his exceptional technical ability and maturity.

timeline.title: Official First-Team Debut
timeline.description: Made his official FC Barcelona debut against Espanyol in La Liga, replacing Deco in the closing stages and becoming the club's youngest player to appear in an official match at that time.

timeline.title: Official First-Team Debut
timeline.description: Made his official FC Barcelona debut against Espanyol in La Liga, replacing Deco.

timeline.title: First Official Goal
timeline.description: Scored his first senior goal against Albacete from Ronaldinho's assist, becoming Barcelona's youngest official goalscorer at the time.

timeline.title: First Major Trophy
timeline.description: Won his first La Liga title with Barcelona.

timeline.title: Champions League Winner
timeline.description: Barcelona won the UEFA Champions League. Messi missed the final through injury but received a winners' medal.

timeline.title: First El Clásico Hat-trick
timeline.description: Scored a hat-trick in a 3-3 draw against Real Madrid, becoming the first player in 12 years to score a Clásico hat-trick.

timeline.title: Pep Guardiola Era Begins
timeline.description: Pep Guardiola became first-team manager, beginning Barcelona's most successful period with Messi as the focal point.

timeline.title: Historic Treble and Sextuple
timeline.description: Barcelona won La Liga, Copa del Rey and UEFA Champions League, then completed the historic sextuple by winning six trophies in one calendar year.

timeline.title: First Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won his first Ballon d'Or after an outstanding season.

timeline.title: First FIFA Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won the inaugural FIFA Ballon d'Or.

timeline.title: Third UEFA Champions League Title
timeline.description: Scored in Barcelona's 3-1 victory over Manchester United in the UEFA Champions League Final at Wembley.

timeline.title: Barcelona All-Time Top Goalscorer
timeline.description: Surpassed César Rodríguez to become FC Barcelona's all-time leading goalscorer.

timeline.title: 91 Goals in a Calendar Year
timeline.description: Set the world record for the most official goals scored in a calendar year with 91.

timeline.title: Second Treble
timeline.description: Barcelona won La Liga, Copa del Rey and UEFA Champions League, completing the club's second continental treble.

timeline.title: Fifth Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won his fifth Ballon d'Or after leading Barcelona to another treble.

timeline.title: Named Club Captain
timeline.description: Succeeded Andrés Iniesta as captain of FC Barcelona.

timeline.title: Most Goals for a Single Club
timeline.description: Surpassed Pelé's record for the most official goals scored for a single club.

timeline.title: Most Appearances for Barcelona
timeline.description: Surpassed Xavi Hernández to become FC Barcelona's all-time appearance leader.

timeline.title: Final Trophy with Barcelona
timeline.description: Scored twice as Barcelona defeated Athletic Club 4-0 to win the Copa del Rey.

timeline.title: Barcelona Departure Announced
timeline.description: FC Barcelona announced that Messi would leave the club because a new contract could not be completed under La Liga's financial regulations.

timeline.title: Farewell Press Conference
timeline.description: Held an emotional farewell press conference at Camp Nou, ending a 21-year association with FC Barcelona.

timeline.title: Signed for Paris Saint-Germain
timeline.description: Joined Paris Saint-Germain on a two-year contract with an option for a third year after leaving FC Barcelona.

timeline.title: PSG Debut
timeline.description: Made his official debut for Paris Saint-Germain against Reims, coming on as a second-half substitute.

timeline.title: First PSG Goal
timeline.description: Scored his first goal for Paris Saint-Germain in a UEFA Champions League victory over Manchester City.

timeline.title: Won Seventh Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won a record seventh Ballon d'Or after leading Argentina to the 2021 Copa América title.

timeline.title: Won Ligue 1
timeline.description: Helped Paris Saint-Germain win the 2021-22 Ligue 1 title.

timeline.title: Won Trophée des Champions
timeline.description: Won the Trophée des Champions after PSG defeated Nantes.

timeline.title: Second Ligue 1 Title
timeline.description: Secured a second consecutive Ligue 1 championship with Paris Saint-Germain.

timeline.title: Final Match for PSG
timeline.description: Played his final match for Paris Saint-Germain against Clermont Foot.

timeline.title: Announced Move to Inter Miami
timeline.description: Confirmed that he would continue his career with Major League Soccer club Inter Miami CF.

timeline.title: Signed for Inter Miami
timeline.description: Inter Miami officially announced the signing of Lionel Messi.

timeline.title: Official Presentation
timeline.description: Was presented to Inter Miami supporters during the 'The Unveil' event at DRV PNK Stadium.

timeline.title: Inter Miami Debut
timeline.description: Made his debut against Cruz Azul and scored a stoppage-time free-kick winner.

timeline.title: Won Leagues Cup
timeline.description: Captained Inter Miami to the first trophy in club history by winning the Leagues Cup.

timeline.title: Won Eighth Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won a record eighth Ballon d'Or following Argentina's 2022 FIFA World Cup triumph.

timeline.title: MLS Single-Match Assist Record
timeline.description: Recorded five assists in one MLS match against the New York Red Bulls, setting a league record.

timeline.title: Won Supporters' Shield
timeline.description: Inter Miami secured the MLS Supporters' Shield after defeating the Columbus Crew.

timeline.title: MLS Regular Season Record
timeline.description: Inter Miami finished the regular season with 74 points, setting a new MLS single-season points record.

timeline.title: Named MLS Most Valuable Player
timeline.description: Won the MLS MVP award after an outstanding 2024 regular season.

timeline.title: Argentina U20 Debut
timeline.description: Made his debut for Argentina's youth national team, beginning his international career.

timeline.title: FIFA World Youth Championship Winner
timeline.description: Led Argentina to victory at the FIFA World Youth Championship in the Netherlands, finishing as both Golden Ball and Golden Boot winner.

timeline.title: Senior Argentina Debut
timeline.description: Made his senior international debut against Hungary.

timeline.title: First Senior International Goal
timeline.description: Scored his first goal for Argentina against Croatia.

timeline.title: First FIFA World Cup
timeline.description: Represented Argentina at the FIFA World Cup in Germany and became the nation's youngest World Cup goalscorer.

timeline.title: Copa América Runner-up
timeline.description: Helped Argentina reach the Copa América Final and was named Best Young Player of the tournament.

timeline.title: Olympic Gold Medal
timeline.description: Won the Olympic gold medal with Argentina at the Beijing Olympic Games.

timeline.title: Named Argentina Captain
timeline.description: Became the permanent captain of the Argentina national team under Alejandro Sabella.

timeline.title: FIFA World Cup Runner-up
timeline.description: Captained Argentina to the FIFA World Cup Final and received the Golden Ball as the tournament's best player.

timeline.title: Copa América Runner-up
timeline.description: Reached the Copa América Final before losing to Chile on penalties.

timeline.title: Argentina's All-Time Top Goalscorer
timeline.description: Broke Gabriel Batistuta's record by scoring against the United States in the Copa América Centenario semi-final.

timeline.title: Announced International Retirement
timeline.description: Announced his retirement from international football after the Copa América Centenario Final loss to Chile.

timeline.title: Returned to Argentina
timeline.description: Reversed his retirement decision and returned to the national team.

timeline.title: Fourth FIFA World Cup
timeline.description: Represented Argentina at the FIFA World Cup in Russia.

timeline.title: Copa América Third Place
timeline.description: Helped Argentina finish third at the Copa América in Brazil.

timeline.title: First Senior International Trophy
timeline.description: Captained Argentina to victory over Brazil in the Copa América Final at the Maracanã and was named Player of the Tournament.

timeline.title: Won the Finalissima
timeline.description: Argentina defeated Italy 3-0 at Wembley, with Messi named Player of the Match.

timeline.title: FIFA World Cup Champion
timeline.description: Captained Argentina to victory over France in the FIFA World Cup Final, scoring twice and winning the Golden Ball for a record second time.

timeline.title: 100 International Goals
timeline.description: Scored a hat-trick against Curaçao to surpass 100 goals for Argentina.

timeline.title: CONMEBOL World Cup Qualifying Record
timeline.description: Became the all-time leading goalscorer in CONMEBOL FIFA World Cup qualification.

timeline.title: Won Copa América 2024
timeline.description: Argentina defeated Colombia after extra time to retain the Copa América title. Messi left the final injured during the second half but lifted his fourth major senior international trophy after the match.

timeline.title: First Official Senior Goal
timeline.description: Scored his first official goal for FC Barcelona against Albacete.

timeline.title: First El Clásico Hat-trick
timeline.description: Scored his first hat-trick against Real Madrid in a 3–3 La Liga draw.

timeline.title: First Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won his first Ballon d'Or after Barcelona's historic sextuple season.

timeline.title: 100 Career Goals
timeline.description: Reached 100 official senior career goals for club and country.

timeline.title: Barcelona's All-Time Leading Goalscorer
timeline.description: Surpassed César Rodríguez to become FC Barcelona's all-time leading scorer.

timeline.title: 91 Goals in a Calendar Year
timeline.description: Set the Guinness World Record for the most official goals scored in a calendar year (91).

timeline.title: Fourth Consecutive Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Became the first player to win four consecutive Ballon d'Or awards.

timeline.title: 400 Career Goals
timeline.description: Reached 400 official senior career goals.

timeline.title: Second Continental Treble
timeline.description: Became the first player to play a key role in two continental trebles with the same club.

timeline.title: 500 Career Goals
timeline.description: Reached 500 official senior career goals.

timeline.title: Sixth European Golden Shoe
timeline.description: Won a record sixth European Golden Shoe.

timeline.title: Most Goals for One Club
timeline.description: Surpassed Pelé's record for the most official goals scored for a single club.

timeline.title: Most International Goals by a South American Man
timeline.description: Broke Pelé's record to become the highest-scoring South American male international footballer.

timeline.title: Completed Football
timeline.description: Won the FIFA World Cup, completing every major team honour available in his senior career.

timeline.title: 100 International Goals
timeline.description: Scored a hat-trick against Curaçao to surpass 100 international goals for Argentina.

timeline.title: Eighth Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Extended his own record by winning an eighth Ballon d'Or.

timeline.title: 1000 Official Senior Matches
timeline.description: Reached the milestone of 1,000 official senior appearances.

timeline.title: Copa América Appearance Record
timeline.description: Became the player with the most appearances in Copa América history.

timeline.title: Second Copa América Title
timeline.description: Won the Copa América for a second time, adding another major international title to his career.

timeline.title: MLS Most Valuable Player
timeline.description: Won the MLS MVP award after leading Inter Miami to the Supporters' Shield and a record-setting regular season.

timeline.title: Golden Boy Award
timeline.description: Won the Golden Boy award, recognizing the best young player in European football.

timeline.title: FIFA World Youth Championship Awards
timeline.description: Won both the Golden Ball and Golden Boot after leading Argentina to the FIFA World Youth Championship title.

timeline.title: First Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won his first Ballon d'Or after Barcelona's historic sextuple season.

timeline.title: FIFA World Player of the Year
timeline.description: Won the FIFA World Player of the Year award.

timeline.title: First FIFA Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won the inaugural FIFA Ballon d'Or after FIFA and France Football merged their awards.

timeline.title: Second FIFA Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Successfully defended the FIFA Ballon d'Or title.

timeline.title: Third FIFA Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won a third consecutive FIFA Ballon d'Or after his record-breaking 91-goal calendar year.

timeline.title: Fourth Consecutive Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Became the first player in history to win four consecutive Ballon d'Or awards.

timeline.title: FIFA World Cup Golden Ball
timeline.description: Won the Golden Ball as the best player of the 2014 FIFA World Cup.

timeline.title: Fifth Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won his fifth Ballon d'Or after Barcelona's second continental treble.

timeline.title: Sixth Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won a record sixth Ballon d'Or.

timeline.title: The Best FIFA Men's Player
timeline.description: Won The Best FIFA Men's Player award.

timeline.title: Laureus World Sportsman of the Year
timeline.description: Became the first footballer to win the Laureus World Sportsman of the Year Award (shared with Lewis Hamilton).

timeline.title: Seventh Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won a record seventh Ballon d'Or after leading Argentina to the Copa América title.

timeline.title: The Best FIFA Men's Player
timeline.description: Won The Best FIFA Men's Player following Argentina's FIFA World Cup triumph.

timeline.title: FIFA World Cup Golden Ball
timeline.description: Became the first player in history to win the FIFA World Cup Golden Ball twice.

timeline.title: Laureus World Sportsman of the Year
timeline.description: Won his second Laureus World Sportsman of the Year award.

timeline.title: Eighth Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Extended his own record by winning an eighth Ballon d'Or.

timeline.title: TIME Athlete of the Year
timeline.description: Named TIME Athlete of the Year after transforming football's popularity in the United States.

timeline.title: MLS Most Valuable Player
timeline.description: Won the Landon Donovan MLS Most Valuable Player award after leading Inter Miami to the Supporters' Shield and a record-breaking MLS regular season.

timeline.title: First Senior Trophy
timeline.achievement: Won La Liga with FC Barcelona.

timeline.title: FIFA World Youth Championship
timeline.achievement: Won the FIFA World Youth Championship with Argentina U20.

timeline.title: First UEFA Champions League
timeline.achievement: Won the UEFA Champions League with Barcelona.

timeline.title: Olympic Gold Medal
timeline.achievement: Won the Men's Olympic Football Tournament with Argentina.

timeline.title: Historic Sextuple
timeline.achievement: Barcelona became the first club to win six official trophies in a single calendar year.

timeline.title: Third UEFA Champions League
timeline.achievement: Won his third UEFA Champions League title.

timeline.title: Second Continental Treble
timeline.achievement: Won La Liga, Copa del Rey and UEFA Champions League in the same season.

timeline.title: 34th Barcelona Trophy
timeline.achievement: Became FC Barcelona's most decorated player.

timeline.title: First Senior International Trophy
timeline.achievement: Won the Copa América after defeating Brazil in the final.

timeline.title: Finalissima Champion
timeline.achievement: Argentina defeated Italy 3–0 at Wembley Stadium.

timeline.title: FIFA World Cup Champion
timeline.achievement: Captained Argentina to victory over France in the FIFA World Cup Final.

timeline.title: Second Ligue 1 Title
timeline.achievement: Won a second consecutive Ligue 1 title with Paris Saint-Germain.

timeline.title: Leagues Cup Champion
timeline.achievement: Led Inter Miami to its first trophy in club history.

timeline.title: Second Copa América
timeline.achievement: Argentina defeated Colombia to retain the Copa América title.

timeline.title: Supporters' Shield Champion
timeline.achievement: Inter Miami won the MLS Supporters' Shield for the first time in club history.

timeline.title: Youngest Barcelona Goalscorer
timeline.description: Became FC Barcelona's youngest official goalscorer at the time after scoring against Albacete.

timeline.title: El Clásico Hat-trick
timeline.description: Became the first player in over a decade to score a hat-trick in El Clásico.

timeline.title: First Sextuple Winner
timeline.description: Helped FC Barcelona become the first club in football history to win six official trophies in a calendar year.

timeline.title: Barcelona All-Time Leading Goalscorer
timeline.description: Surpassed César Rodríguez as FC Barcelona's all-time top scorer.

timeline.title: 91 Goals in One Calendar Year
timeline.description: Set the Guinness World Record for the most official goals scored in a calendar year.

timeline.title: 50 La Liga Goals in One Season
timeline.description: Set the record for the most goals in a single La Liga season.

timeline.title: Four Consecutive Ballon d'Or Awards
timeline.description: Became the first player in football history to win four consecutive Ballon d'Or awards.

timeline.title: Champions League All-Time Top Scorer
timeline.description: Became the UEFA Champions League's all-time leading scorer (a record later surpassed by Cristiano Ronaldo).

timeline.title: Two Continental Trebles
timeline.description: Became the first player to play a key role in winning two continental trebles with the same club.

timeline.title: Argentina's All-Time Leading Goalscorer
timeline.description: Surpassed Gabriel Batistuta's international goals record.

timeline.title: Most Decorated Barcelona Player
timeline.description: Became the most successful player in FC Barcelona history by trophies won.

timeline.title: Most Goals for a Single Club
timeline.description: Surpassed Pelé's record for the most official goals scored for one club.

timeline.title: Most Barcelona Appearances
timeline.description: Surpassed Xavi Hernández to become FC Barcelona's all-time appearance leader.

timeline.title: South America's Top International Goalscorer
timeline.description: Surpassed Pelé to become the highest-scoring South American men's international footballer.

timeline.title: Two FIFA World Cup Golden Balls
timeline.description: Became the first player to win the FIFA World Cup Golden Ball award twice.

timeline.title: Most World Cup Minutes Played
timeline.description: Set the all-time FIFA World Cup record for minutes played.

timeline.title: Most World Cup Appearances
timeline.description: Broke the all-time FIFA World Cup appearance record by reaching 26 matches.

timeline.title: 100 International Goals
timeline.description: Became the third men's footballer and the first South American to score 100 international goals.

timeline.title: CONMEBOL World Cup Qualifying Record
timeline.description: Became the all-time leading goalscorer in CONMEBOL FIFA World Cup qualification.

timeline.title: Most Copa América Appearances
timeline.description: Became the player with the most appearances in Copa América history.

timeline.title: Most Copa América Knockout Matches
timeline.description: Set the record for the most Copa América knockout-stage appearances.

timeline.title: Born
timeline.description: Born in Rosario, Santa Fe, Argentina.

timeline.title: Barcelona Agreement
timeline.description: FC Barcelona agreed to sign Messi through the famous 'napkin contract', securing his move to Spain and funding his medical treatment.

timeline.title: Professional Debut
timeline.description: Made his official first-team debut for FC Barcelona against Espanyol in La Liga.

timeline.title: First Professional Goal
timeline.description: Scored his first official goal for FC Barcelona against Albacete.

timeline.title: Argentina Senior Debut
timeline.description: Made his senior international debut for Argentina against Hungary.

timeline.title: Olympic Champion
timeline.description: Won the Olympic gold medal with Argentina at the Beijing Olympic Games.

timeline.title: World's Best Player
timeline.description: Won his first Ballon d'Or and helped Barcelona complete the historic sextuple.

timeline.title: Argentina Captain
timeline.description: Became the permanent captain of the Argentina national team.

timeline.title: Barcelona All-Time Top Scorer
timeline.description: Became FC Barcelona's all-time leading goalscorer.

timeline.title: 91 Goals in a Calendar Year
timeline.description: Set the world record with 91 official goals in a single calendar year.

timeline.title: Second European Treble
timeline.description: Won his second continental treble with Barcelona.

timeline.title: Argentina's Leading Goalscorer
timeline.description: Surpassed Gabriel Batistuta to become Argentina's all-time top goalscorer.

timeline.title: Barcelona Captain
timeline.description: Succeeded Andrés Iniesta as captain of FC Barcelona.

timeline.title: Most Goals for One Club
timeline.description: Broke Pelé's record for the most official goals scored for a single club.

timeline.title: Barcelona Appearance Record
timeline.description: Became FC Barcelona's all-time appearance leader.

timeline.title: First Senior International Trophy
timeline.description: Captained Argentina to the Copa América title after defeating Brazil in the final.

timeline.title: Paris Saint-Germain Transfer
timeline.description: Signed for Paris Saint-Germain after leaving Barcelona.

timeline.title: Finalissima Champion
timeline.description: Argentina defeated Italy 3–0 at Wembley.

timeline.title: World Cup Champion
timeline.description: Captained Argentina to victory in the FIFA World Cup Final against France, completing the final major trophy missing from his career.

timeline.title: Joined Inter Miami
timeline.description: Signed with Major League Soccer club Inter Miami CF.

timeline.title: First Inter Miami Trophy
timeline.description: Led Inter Miami to the Leagues Cup, the first trophy in club history.

timeline.title: Eighth Ballon d'Or
timeline.description: Won a record eighth Ballon d'Or.

timeline.title: Second Copa América
timeline.description: Won his second Copa América title as Argentina defeated Colombia in the final.

timeline.title: Supporters' Shield Champion
timeline.description: Helped Inter Miami win the MLS Supporters' Shield with a record-breaking regular season.

events: Born on 24 June in Rosario, Santa Fe, Argentina.
Spent his early childhood with his family in Rosario.
Developed a growing passion for football.
Continued playing football recreationally with family and friends.
Started playing organized football for Grandoli under his father Jorge Messi.
Recognized locally as one of Rosario's most talented young footballers.
Joined the youth academy of Newell's Old Boys.
Became a key player in Newell's famous 'Machine of '87' youth team.
Won numerous local youth tournaments with Newell's Old Boys.
Attracted attention from clubs in Argentina and Europe.
Traveled to Barcelona for a trial.
Moved to Spain and joined La Masia.
Progressed rapidly through Barcelona's youth teams.
Made his unofficial first-team debut against FC Porto.
Won the FIFA World Youth Championship.
Played in his first FIFA World Cup.
Scored his first El Clásico hat-trick.
Won the inaugural FIFA Ballon d'Or.
Won his third consecutive FIFA Ballon d'Or.
Scored 91 goals in a calendar year.
Won his fourth consecutive Ballon d'Or.
Won a fourth consecutive Ballon d'Or.
Reached the FIFA World Cup Final.
Won the FIFA World Cup Golden Ball.
Returned to the Argentina national team.
Qualified Argentina for the 2018 FIFA World Cup with a hat-trick against Ecuador.
Played at the FIFA World Cup in Russia.
Won The Best FIFA Men's Player award.
Broke Pelé's record for most goals for a single club.
Won the FIFA World Cup Golden Ball.
Became the all-time Copa América appearance leader.

career_journey.title: Early Life
career_journey.description: Born in Rosario, developed at Grandoli and Newell's Old Boys before moving to Spain.

career_journey.title: La Masia
career_journey.description: Joined FC Barcelona's academy, progressed rapidly through every youth level and reached the first team.

career_journey.title: Barcelona Era
career_journey.description: Established himself as one of football's greatest players, winning 35 trophies and becoming Barcelona's all-time leader in appearances, goals and assists.

career_journey.title: Paris Saint-Germain
career_journey.description: Won two Ligue 1 titles and one Trophée des Champions before leaving France.

career_journey.title: Inter Miami
career_journey.description: Led Inter Miami to its first trophies while continuing to break records in Major League Soccer.

career_journey.title: Argentina
career_journey.description: Won the FIFA World Youth Championship, Olympic Gold Medal, Copa América (2021 & 2024), Finalissima and the 2022 FIFA World Cup.

historical_significance: Most Ballon d'Or awards in football history.
Most FIFA World Cup minutes played.
Most goals for a single club.
Only player to win two FIFA World Cup Golden Balls.
Only captain to lead Argentina to the Copa América, Finalissima and FIFA World Cup in the modern era.

legacy.description: By the end of 2024, Lionel Messi had completed one of the most decorated and influential careers in football history. Across more than two decades of professional football, he established numerous records, won every major team honour available to him, and earned widespread recognition as one of the greatest players ever to play the sport.
